title: Choose Display from Formatting Behavior
summary: Build and verify css display flow in survey action and help-text flow repair through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css display flow from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css display flow without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey artifact and connect styles.css. -->

options.text: Survey action and help-text flow repair case 2: display none hides content visually while preserving it for assistive technology.
Survey action and help-text flow repair case 2: choose block, inline, inline-block, flow-root, or none from layout participation and accessibility consequences, then inspect both trees.
Survey action and help-text flow repair case 2: set every element to inline-block so normal document flow no longer matters.

steps.title: 04 · Read the survey action and help-text flow repair case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves css display flow when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: survey action and help-text flow repair.
steps.why: Choose Display from Formatting Behavior makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in survey action and help-text flow repair.

content.text: Block boxes participate in block flow, inline boxes flow inside lines, inline-block combines inline placement with box dimensions, and flow-root establishes a new block formatting context. Display none removes an element from layout and the accessibility tree. Display choice should follow formatting and access requirements, not a desire to make arbitrary elements behave like controls. Apply it to survey action and help-text flow repair.

hints: Choose Display from Formatting Behavior checkpoint 4: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Choose Display from Formatting Behavior checkpoint 4: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Choose Display from Formatting Behavior checkpoint 4: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Survey action and help-text flow repair case 4: display none hides content visually while preserving it for assistive technology.
Survey action and help-text flow repair case 4: choose block, inline, inline-block, flow-root, or none from layout participation and accessibility consequences, then inspect both trees.
Survey action and help-text flow repair case 4: set every element to inline-block so normal document flow no longer matters.

options.text: Survey action and help-text flow repair case 5: display none hides content visually while preserving it for assistive technology.

Survey action and help-text flow repair case 5: use display contents on every wrapper because semantics always survive unchanged.

options.text: Survey action and help-text flow repair case 6: keep the survey action and help-text flow repair defect because the happy path worked once.

Survey action and help-text flow repair case 6: a plausible success state is incomplete evidence. Choose block, inline, inline-block, flow-root, or none from layout participation and accessibility consequences, then inspect both trees.
Survey action and help-text flow repair case 6: replace the complete artifact before isolating the failed relationship.
